summary: Install-time beacon to bare-IP HTTP endpoint in React hook package
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script `curl -s http://8.140.205.78` that fires automatically on `npm install`. The destination is a bare IPv4 address over plaintext HTTP, unrelated to the package's stated purpose (a React debounce/throttle hook). This unconditionally phones home on every install, leaking the installer's source IP and install timing to an attacker-controlled host with no opt-out. The package name also mimics common React hook libraries and lists itself as its own dependency, consistent with a typosquat lure whose primary effect on install is the beacon. The current request body is empty and the response is discarded, but the channel is attacker-controllable and can be repurposed at any time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
